title: mentha gentilis var. pugetii
intro: Mentha gentilis var. pugetii, also known as Oregon Mountain Mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha gentilis plant, a type of mint native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Oregon Mountain Mint include reducing nausea and vomiting, alleviating headaches, and soothing sore throats, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and anti-viral properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used externally or internally to harness its therapeutic benefits.
